title: Fort Aguada
tagline: Fort Aguada — heritage city of Goa

overview.short: Fort Aguada is a seventeenth-century Portuguese-era fort, built in 1612, along with a lighthouse, standing in Goa, India, on Sinquerim Beach, overlooking the Arabian Sea.
overview.description: Fort Aguada is a seventeenth-century Portuguese-era fort, built in 1612, along with a lighthouse, standing in Goa, India, on Sinquerim Beach, overlooking the Arabian Sea. It is an ASI protected Monument of National Importance in Goa.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Taj Fort Aguada Resort
topPlaces.description: Taj Fort Aguada Resort & Spa, formerly Fort Aguada Beach Resort, is built on the crescent of the beach at Sinquerim, in Goa, India. The hotel is part of the chain hotels of Taj Group of Hotels.

topPlaces.name: Sinquerim
topPlaces.description: Sinquerim is a village in Bardez sub-district, North Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Panaji
topPlaces.description: Lok Bhavan, formerly Raj Bhavan (Government House) is a residential palace and fort situated in Dona Paula, Goa, which serves as the official residence of the governor of Goa. The sprawling 88 acres estate is located on a cape in the Goan capital, Panaji.

topPlaces.name: Miramar, Goa
topPlaces.description: Miramar is the beach area of the Goan capital of Panjim, also known as Panaji and is one of the most visited beaches of Goa. It is one of the two only beaches in Panjim, other being Caranzalem beach.

topPlaces.name: Reis Magos
topPlaces.description: Reis Magos is a village located on the northern bank of the Mandovi River in Bardez, Goa, opposite to the capital city of Panjim. The village is famous for two of Goa’s famous structures; the Reis Magos Fort, and the Reis Magos Church – the first church in Bardez.

topPlaces.name: Candolim
topPlaces.description: Candolim is a census town in North Goa and is located in the Bardez taluka in the state of Goa, India. It is situated just south of Calangute Beach and North of Sinquerim.

topPlaces.name: Campal Indoor Complex
topPlaces.description: Campal Indoor Complex is a multi-purposed indoor stadium in Campal, Goa, India. The stadium is a main stadium in the town of Campal.

topPlaces.name: Mandovi River
topPlaces.description: The Mandovi (IPA: Konkani pronunciation: [maːɳɖɔwĩː]) or Mahadayi (IPA: Kannada pronunciation: [məɦəd̪ɑːji]), formerly known as the Rio de Goa, is a river described as the lifeline of the Indian state of Goa. The Mandovi and the Zuari are the two principal rivers in the state of Goa.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Taj Fort Aguada Resort & around

items.note: Taj Fort Aguada Resort & Spa, formerly Fort Aguada Beach Resort, is built on the crescent of the beach at Sinquerim, in Goa, India. The hotel is part of the chain hotels of Taj Group of Hotels.
Sinquerim is a village in Bardez sub-district, North Goa, India.

Lok Bhavan, formerly Raj Bhavan (Government House) is a residential palace and fort situated in Dona Paula, Goa, which serves as the official residence of the governor of Goa. The sprawling 88 acres estate is located on a cape in the Goan capital, Panaji.
Miramar is the beach area of the Goan capital of Panjim, also known as Panaji and is one of the most visited beaches of Goa. It is one of the two only beaches in Panjim, other being Caranzalem beach.

Reis Magos is a village located on the northern bank of the Mandovi River in Bardez, Goa, opposite to the capital city of Panjim. The village is famous for two of Goa’s famous structures; the Reis Magos Fort, and the Reis Magos Church – the first church in Bardez.
Candolim is a census town in North Goa and is located in the Bardez taluka in the state of Goa, India. It is situated just south of Calangute Beach and North of Sinquerim.
Campal Indoor Complex is a multi-purposed indoor stadium in Campal, Goa, India. The stadium is a main stadium in the town of Campal.

The Mandovi (IPA: Konkani pronunciation: [maːɳɖɔwĩː]) or Mahadayi (IPA: Kannada pronunciation: [məɦəd̪ɑːji]), formerly known as the Rio de Goa, is a river described as the lifeline of the Indian state of Goa. The Mandovi and the Zuari are the two principal rivers in the state of Goa.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fort Aguada?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fort Aguada is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fort Aguada?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (11 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fort Aguada?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fort Aguada at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fort Aguada?
faq.a: Stays in Fort Aguada start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fort Aguada?
faq.a: The top attractions are Taj Fort Aguada Resort, Sinquerim, Lok Bhavan, Panaji, Miramar, Goa, Reis Magos.
